titleStmt.title: Letter from Robert Brown to Sir Robert Chalmers, 2 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Robert Brown

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Robert Brown to Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938). The letter concerns a payment that Brown feels is owed to him. He writes to Chalmers to express his frustrations about not being paid in accordance to what he was promised by the police officials. In the letter, he asks if he could direct his claims, instead, to someone who would be able to process his payment.Robert Brown had appeared at as a witness at a trial. Robert Brown, the assistant manager at the National Margarine Company Ltd. Before the war, a middle-class British household tended to put butter on the table, and reserve margarine for cooking purposes. But because the rations for margarine were more generous than for butter, margarine worked its way out of the kitchen and into the dining room. Sir Robert Chalmers was sent to Ireland as under-secretary, replacing Sir Matthew Nathan who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, as the chief secretary following the Easter Rising in 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/9471/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
The National Margarine Company
Limited. 10, Quality Street, Leith 2nd. June 1916
To The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle, Dublin. Dear Sir,
I am in receipt of your letter of 30th.inst for which
I thank you.
I have read over your various letters and I am unable to
gather from them your reason for refusing to give instructions for
payment of the balance of my claim.
You give different reasons in different letters and there
seems no valid reason why I should not be paid in accordance with t
the promise made by the Police Officials. If Mr. J.J.Taylor who
signs your leters has not the power to order payment then I shall
be glad if you will inform me to whom I should now submit my claim. Yours truly, Rob Brown
Ack,, and state that T.E can only
report that they leave nothing to add
to the replies but to his previous applications
E.O.F. 5 â 6 â 16
If undelivered return to:- THE NATIONAL MARGARINE CO., Ltd., 10 QUALITY STREET, LEITH.
